title: Using ontoProc2 with Gene Ontology
text: begin filtering semantic SQL representation GO remove explicitly deprecated terms. Now isolate IDs presented GO.db non-deprecated IDs semantic SQL. ’s one example: ’s example term semantic SQL GO.db: discussion ‘obsoleting’ GO:0018942 https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/27153 illuminates negotiations needed term exclusion/inclusion.
code: godep = tbl(gg, "statements") |> filter(str_like(subject, "GO:%"), (predicate == "owl:deprecated")) |>      select(subject) |> distinct() |> pull()  semgo = tbl(gg, "statements") |> filter(str_like(subject, "GO:%")) |> select(subject) |>     distinct() |> pull() gok = setdiff(semgo, godep)  # non-deprecated ids in semantic SQL inbioconly = setdiff(keys(GO.db), gok) ibco = AnnotationDbi::select(GO.db, keys=inbioconly, columns=c("GOID", "TERM")) ## 'select()' returned 1:1 mapping between keys and columns dim(ibco) ## [1] 1257    2 datatable(head(ibco)) tmp = ibco[1,"GOID"] tbl(gg, "statements") |> select(subject, predicate, object, value) |>     filter(subject == tmp) |> as.data.frame() |> datatable() insemonly = setdiff(gok, keys(GO.db)) tmp = insemonly[1] tbl(gg, "statements") |> select(subject, predicate, object, value) |>     filter(subject == tmp) |> as.data.frame() |> datatable()

    SELECT

      sl.value AS subject_label,
      e.predicate,
      pl.value AS predicate_label,
      e.object,
      ol.value AS object_label
    FROM edge e
    LEFT JOIN rdfs_label_statement sl ON e.subject = sl.subject
    LEFT JOIN rdfs_label_statement pl ON e.predicate = pl.subject
    LEFT JOIN rdfs_label_statement ol ON e.object = ol.subject
    WHERE %s

title: ontoProc2 -- leveraging semantic SQL for ontology analysis in Bioconductor
text: INCAtools notebook includes example finding neurons part forebrain. involves identifying CURIEs relations anatomical structures, thus working relational ontology (RO) UBERON. First question: ’s CURIE “forebrain” UBERON? Second question: ’s CURIE “soma location” RO? ’s CURIE “neuron”? Now use three steps obtain solution. First, enumerate cell types located forebrain. Second, filter identified ‘subclassOf’ “neuron”. Finally, get labels.
code: ub = semsql_connect(ontology="uberon") ## Connected to SemanticSQL database: /home/vincent/.cache/R/BiocFileCache/b87946edce89_uberon.db ## Primary ontology prefix: UBERON ro = semsql_connect(ontology="ro") ## Connected to SemanticSQL database: /home/vincent/.cache/R/BiocFileCache/1269f106cdd77_ro.db ## Primary ontology prefix: RO fbcur = search_labels(ub, "forebrain", limit=1000) |> filter(label=="forebrain") |>   select(subject) |> unlist() fbcur ##          subject  ## "UBERON:0001890" loccur = search_labels(ro, "has soma location") |> select(subject) |> unlist() loccur ##      subject  ## "RO:0002100" ncur = search_labels(clss, "neuron", limit=1000) |> filter(label=="neuron") |>   select(subject) |> unlist() ncur ##      subject  ## "CL:0000540" clinfb = tbl(clss@con, "entailed_edge") |> filter(predicate == loccur, object == fbcur) |>           select(subject) |> collect() |> unlist()  length(clinfb) ## [1] 185 clisneur = tbl(clss@con, "entailed_edge") |> filter(predicate == "rdfs:subClassOf", object==ncur) |>           filter(subject %in% clinfb) |> select(subject) |> collect() |> unlist()  length(clisneur) ## [1] 185 tbl(clss@con, "rdfs_label_statement") |> filter(subject %in% clisneur) |>           select(subject, value) |> collect() |> DT::datatable()

what: SemsqlConn: S7 connection wrapper for SemanticSQL databases — SemsqlConn
title: SemsqlConn: S7 connection wrapper for SemanticSQL databases — SemsqlConn
text: S7 class encapsulates SQLite connection ontology database following SemanticSQL schema. Provides methods common ontology queries including label lookup, ancestor/descendant traversal, relationship queries. Properties: con DBI connection object (SQLiteConnection) db_path character(1) path SQLite database file ontology_prefix character(1) primary ontology prefix,     e.g. "CL" Cell Ontology

The execution phase is the last step of an apoptotic process, and is typically characterized by rounding-up of the cell, retraction of pseudopodes, reduction of cellular volume (pyknosis), chromatin condensation, nuclear fragmentation (karyorrhexis), plasma membrane blebbing and fragmentation of the cell into apoptotic bodies. title: Create a SemsqlConn connection — semsql_connect
text: Opens connection SemanticSQL SQLite database, either supplying direct file path referencing short ontology name retrieved cached via BiocFileCache.

title: Create a SemsqlConn connection — semsql_connect
code: semsql_connect(   db_path = NULL,   ontology_prefix = NULL,   ontology = NULL,   cache = BiocFileCache::BiocFileCache(),   ... )

title: Create a SemsqlConn connection — semsql_connect
text: db_path character(1) NULL. Path existing SQLite database file. Either db_path ontology must supplied. ontology_prefix character(1) NULL. Primary CURIE prefix ontology (e.g. "CL"). NULL ontology supplied, defaults toupper(ontology); otherwise auto-detected database. ontology character(1) NULL. Short name INCAtools ontology (e.g. "cl", "go"). supplied, retrieve_semsql_conn called locate download cached database. cache BiocFileCache instance used ontology supplied. Defaults BiocFileCache::BiocFileCache(). ... passed retrieve_semsql_conn ultimately download.file.
